summary: eth-dev decrypts and executes bundled payload on require with cross-platform persistence
details: index.js exports Ethereum helper functions but appends an IIFE that runs on require(). After a ~42 second delay it AES-256-CBC-decrypts test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a 53 KB base64 blob) using a hardcoded key and IV, writes the plaintext JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js, and executes it with a detached node process. The loader also installs OS-specific persistence for the decrypted payload: a crontab entry on Linux invoking syncd.js every 12 hours, a Windows scheduled task named "WinNodeSync" via schtasks, and a macOS LaunchAgent at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.apple.syncd.plist masquerading as an Apple system service. The encrypted payload is disguised as a test fixture but is loaded from the production import path, not from tests. The decrypted bytecode is opaque and attacker-controlled.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
